content.text: Example using fetch and readable stream

## About

CSS-Parser is a high-performance, fault-tolerant, and dependency-free CSS toolkit for Node.js and browsers.

It implements the [CSS Syntax Module Level 3](https://www.w3.org/TR/css-syntax-3/) specification and validates CSS using syntax rules from [MDN Data](https://github.com/mdn/data).

In addition to parsing and validation, CSS-Parser provides advanced optimization and minification capabilities. According to [this benchmark](https://tbela99.github.io/css-parser/benchmark/index.html), it is the most efficient CSS minifier available, producing smaller output than competing solutions while maintaining competitive performance.

## Playground

Want to see it in action? Try the [CSS-Parser playground](https://tbela99.github.io/css-parser/playground/).

## Features

A non-exhaustive list of features is provided below:


* **Zero dependencies** — lightweight and easy to integrate into any project.
* **Standards-based CSS validation** powered by MDN data.
* **Full CSS Modules support** for modern component-based workflows.
* **Fault-tolerant parsing** that follows the CSS Syntax Module Level 3 specification.
* **High-performance minification** with safe optimizations and no unsafe transforms.
* **Advanced color processing** with support for modern color spaces and functions, including

, system colors, and relative colors.
* **Color conversion engine** capable of transforming colors between all supported formats.
* **Automatic CSS nesting generation** from compatible selectors.
* **Nested CSS transpilation** to legacy-compatible syntax.
* **

function transpilation** for broader browser compatibility.
* **Source map generation** for easier debugging and development workflows.
* **Shorthand property computation** to reduce output size and improve optimization.
* **Transform function optimization** for more compact CSS output.
* **Math function evaluation**, including

, and related expressions.
* **CSS variable inlining** where values can be safely resolved.
* **Duplicate declaration removal** to eliminate redundant rules.
* **

flattening** to produce self-contained stylesheets.
* **Experimental vendor prefix cleanup** to modernize generated CSS.

## Installation

### From NPM

### As Web module

It can be imported as an umd module.

### As UMD module

It can be imported as a module in the browser.

The library exposes three entry points:

-

which relies on node fs and fs/promises to read files

which relies on the web fetch api to read files

Load as javascript module

## Main function differences

| Function | Parses CSS | Generates CSS Output |
|----------|------------|----------------------|
|

only produce the AST and does not generate CSS output.

By contrast,
parses the CSS **and** generates the transformed CSS text. This is useful when you want the rendered CSS directly without performing a separate AST rendering step.

;

const result = await transform(css); // or transform(

````
## Parsing

Parsing converts input CSS into an **AST (Abstract Syntax Tree)**.

You can parse CSS in two ways:

- `parse()` – Parses CSS and returns an AST.
- `transform()` – Parses and generate CSS as part of the transformation process.

For more information about the available parsing options, see the TypeScript documentation for [`ParserOptions`](../interfaces/node.ParserOptions.html).

### Usage

```javascript
parse(css, parserOptions = {})
parse(parserOptions = {input: css})
```

### Example

````

## Validation

Validation is powered by [mdn-data](https://github.com/mdn/data) and can be enabled using the [

— Validates declaration nodes only when they are used as shorthand properties.

CSS rules and at-rules are always validated, regardless of the
setting. The option only controls whether CSS declarations are checked against their syntax definitions for correctness. Validation does not affect the resulting CSS.

## Lenient validation

The parser is lenient. This means that unknown at-rules and declarations are preserved by default.
This behavior is changed using the [lenient](

## Ast nodes validation state

Validation results are available through the node's
property, while detailed validation errors can be accessed from the node's

property.

The following example prints the validation state of each node, along with any validation error details.

## Overriding Node Validation State

A node's validation state can be overridden using node visitors. The
In the example below, the node's validation state is changed from
, causing the node to be preserved in the output instead of being discarded.

# CSS Modules

CSS module is a feature that allows you to use CSS classes in a way that is safe from conflicts with other classes in the same project.
To enable CSS module support, pass the

option to the parse() or transform() function.
For a detailed explanation of the module options, see the [module options](

option is used to configure the scope of the generated class names.

### Local scope

This is the default scope.

### Global scope

The class names are not scoped unless they are scoped using :local or :local()

### ICSS scope

Export CSS using ICSS format

### Pure scope

Require the use at least one id or class in selectors. It will throw an error it there are no id or class name in the selector.

### Shortest Scope

Produce short scope names.

### Mixing scopes

Scopes can be mixed using the bitwise OR operator '|'

## Class composition

Class composition is supported using the
Classes can be composed from other files as well as the global scope

option is used to configure the case of the generated class names as well as the class mapping. 

### Ignore Case

No case transformation

### Camel case

Use camel case for the mapping key names

### Camel case only

Use camel case key names and the scoped class names.

### Dash case

Use dash case for the mapping key names

### Dash case only

Use dash case key names and the scoped class names.
option is used to configure the generated scoped names.

The supported placeholders are:
- name: the file base name without the extension
- hash: the file path hash
- local: the local name
- path: the file path
- folder: the folder name
- ext: the file extension

The pattern placeholders can optionally have a maximum number of characters:
The hash pattern can take an algorithm, a maximum number of characters or both:

Supported hash algorithms are:
- base64
- hex
- base64url
- sha1
- sha256
- sha384
- sha512
------

[← Validation](

## Minification

The minification process is enabled by default. The minification process is applied to the ast as well as the generated CSS code.
It can be disabled by setting

in the options.

Flags are used to control specific minification features.

### Keyframes

Keyframes rules are minified.

### CSS variables inlining

This feature is disabled by default.
It is enabled using

.
The CSS variables must be defined only once, and they must be defined in 'html' or ':root' selectors.

### Math functions

This feature is enabled by default. It is disabled using

### CSS Gradient functions

CSS gradients are minified.

### Color minification

CSS colors level 4&5 are fully supported. The library will convert between all supported color formats.
It can also compute relative colors and color-mix() functions.
The color result color format can be specified using the

### CSS transform functions

This feature is enabled by default. It computes CSS transform functions and preserve the shortest value. It is disabled using

### CSS values

Dimensions and numeric values are minified.

### Redundant declarations

By default, only the last declaration is preserved.
To preserve all declarations, pass the option
To preserve only specific declarations, pass an array of declaration names.

### Calc() resolution

Calc() functions are evaluated.

### CSS variable inlining and relative color

This feature is disabled by default.

### Merging adjacent rules

Adjacent rules with common declarations and at-rules with the same name and prelude are merged.

### Conditional wrapping or unwrapping selectors using :is()

This feature is enabled by default. It is disabled by turning off minification using

.
It will attempt to wrap or unwrap rules using :is() and use the shortest possible selector.

### CSS nesting

This feature is enabled by default. it is disabled using

.
When enabled, css rules are automatically nested whenever possible.

### CSS prefix removal (Experimental)

This feature is disabled by default.

### Shorthands

Shorthand properties are computed and default values are removed.

### Computed shorthands properties

Below is the list of computed shorthands properties:

- [ ] ~all~
- [x] animation
- [x] background
- [x] border
- [ ] border-block-end
- [ ] border-block-start
- [x] border-bottom
- [x] border-color
- [ ] border-image
- [ ] border-inline-end
- [ ] border-inline-start
- [x] border-left
- [x] border-radius
- [x] border-right
- [x] border-style
- [x] border-top
- [x] border-width
- [x] column-rule
- [x] columns
- [x] container
- [ ] contain-intrinsic-size
- [x] flex
- [x] flex-flow
- [x] font
- [ ] font-synthesis
- [ ] font-variant
- [x] gap
- [ ] grid
- [ ] grid-area
- [ ] grid-column
- [ ] grid-row
- [ ] grid-template
- [x] inset
- [x] list-style
- [x] margin
- [ ] mask
- [ ] offset
- [x] outline
- [x] overflow
- [x] padding
- [ ] place-content
- [ ] place-items
- [ ] place-self
- [ ] scroll-margin
- [ ] scroll-padding
- [ ] scroll-timeline
- [x] text-decoration
- [x] text-emphasis
- [x] transition

### UTF-8 escape sequence

UTF-8 escape sequences are decoded and replaced by their corresponding characters.

## Custom transform

Visitors are used to transform the ast tree produced by the parser. For more information about the visitor object see the [typescript definition](

## At-rule visitor

Example: change media at-rule prelude

### Declaration visitor

Example: add 'width: 3px' everytime a declaration with the name 'height' is found

### Rule visitor

Example: add 'width: 3px' to every rule with the selector '.foo'

### KeyframesRule visitor

Keyframes rule visitor is called on each keyframes rule node.

### KeyframesAtRule visitor

The keyframes at-rule visitor is called on each keyframes at-rule node. The visitor can be a function or an object with a property named after the keyframes at-rule prelude.

### Value visitor

The value visitor is called on each token of the selector node, declaration value and the at-rule prelude, etc.

### Generic visitor

Generic token visitor is a function whose name is a keyof [EnumToken](
). it is called for every token of the specified type.

### Example of visitor that inlines images

A visitor that inlines all images under a specific size

New CSS syntax is lowered to legacy syntax.


## CSS if() syntax

Expand CSS if() syntax into legacy syntax.

```css
table.colortable {
 color: color(srgb .787974 .417034 .141379)
}
````

## CSS nesting

Expanded into legacy syntax.

```

## Ast node types

The ast root node returned by the parser is always a [AstStyleSheet](

property for the other node types.

### Ast rule value

[Ast rule](
) value is the selector parsed as the  _tokens_ pwhich is an array of [Token](

) .
The _sel_ attribute is the string representation of the node selector.
Modifying the sel attribute does not affect the tokens attribute, and similarly, changes to the tokens attribute do not
update the sel attribute.

) _tokens_ is either null or an array
of [Token](

) representing the node's prelude.
The _val_ attribute is the string representation of the node's value.

Modifying the _val_ attribute does not affect the _tokens_ attribute, and similarly, changes to the _tokens_ attribute do not
update the _val_ attribute.

) _nam_ attribute is the declaration name. the _val_
attribute is an array of [Token](

## Ast Traversal


### walk()

Ast traversal is achieved using [walk()](
Ast traversal can be controlled using a [filter](
) function. the filter function returns a value of type [WalkerOption](

).
if the filter function returns new nodes, those will also be visited.
) function traverses the value tokens of a node, allowing you to inspect or modify each token during traversal.

### find()

Searches the AST and returns the first node that matches the specified criteria.

### findByValue()

Searches the AST by traversing each node's value tokens and returns the first node with a matching value token.

### findAll()

Searches the AST and returns all nodes that match the specified criteria.

### findLast()

Search the ast tree and return the last node matching the specified criteria.

### findValue()

Find the node's value token of the specified ast node that matches the specified criteria.
) replaced a node in the specified parent. Throws an error if the target node is not found in the parent.

Parse a CSS string using [parseString()]().
